mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal aggressive monetary accommodation that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset valuations. This mechanism operates continuously whenever the Fed maintains ultra-low rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20-day horizons.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.1% on 2020-08-01 indicates emergency accommodation
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% is not statistically significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 8% signals labor market deterioration that triggers risk-off sentiment and equity selloffs. The mechanism activates when UNRATE crosses above 8.0% and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 8.4% on 2020-08-01 reflects pandemic labor shock
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return following UNRATE > 8.0% crossings is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or negative returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth measured by RSAFS accelerates month-over-month, signaling robust consumer demand that supports corporate earnings expectations. This always-on mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20-day horizons when RSAFS shows positive momentum relative to its 3-month moving average.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 535982M on 2020-07-01 reflects post-lockdown demand recovery
failure_condition: Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or correlation reverses sign in more than 40% of rolling 6-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index INDPRO declines below 96.0 indicates supply-side weakness and capacity constraints that compress profit margins and reduce equity valuations. The mechanism triggers when INDPRO falls below 96.0 and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60-day horizons as investors recognize structural supply limitations.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 95.9549 on 2020-08-01 reflects pandemic production collapse
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return when INDPRO < 96.0 is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts measured by HOUST and retail sales RSAFS jointly signal demand strength when both series rise simultaneously. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20-day horizons only when both HOUST and RSAFS show month-over-month growth, capturing broad-based consumer and construction demand.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both increase month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1496K and RSAFS at 535982M on 2020-07-01 show concurrent recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with synchronized demand rebound
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return when both HOUST and RSAFS rise is not significantly higher than when only one rises (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC of interaction term is below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment PAYEMS and unemployment rate UNRATE move in opposite directions, with rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE signaling labor market tightening that supports equity valuations through wage growth and consumer spending. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 20-day horizons when employment gains accelerate while unemployment declines.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases > 200K month-over-month AND UNRATE declines > 0.2pp month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 140914K and UNRATE at 8.4% on 2020-08-01 show ongoing labor recovery
regime_hint: labor market recovery with accelerating job creation
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return when both conditions are met is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05), or the interaction effect explains less than 5% of variance beyond individual series effects.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rises above 2.5% while federal funds rate FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, creating negative real rates that incentivize equity investment as a hedge against currency debasement. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive S&P 500 returns over 60-day horizons only during monetary accommodation with rising inflation.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 259.681 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% on 2020-08-01 show negative real rates
regime_hint: monetary accommodation with inflation expectations rising
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return when both conditions are met is not significantly higher than when only one is met (t-test p > 0.05), or the mechanism reverses direction in more than 30% of rolling 12-month periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth GDPC1 declines quarter-over-quarter, signaling economic contraction that reduces corporate earnings and equity valuations. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60-day horizons when GDP growth turns negative, with the effect amplified during post-shock recovery periods when growth expectations are fragile.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth rate becomes negative
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 17282.188B on 2020-04-01 reflects pandemic contraction
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with fragile growth expectations
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward return following negative GDPC1 growth is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05), or positive returns occur in more than 55% of triggered instances over any 2-year rolling window.
